zh.of Altrincham, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾阿爾特林哈姆
zh.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Massey de Pontington於大約1365年出生在英國英格蘭爾普丁頓時，他的父親Hamon de Massey34歲，母親Jane de Timperley31歲。他和Johanna de Coghall至少有12個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1403年7月21日在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Massey de Pontington于大约1365年出生在英国英格兰爾普丁頓时，他的父亲Hamon de Massey34岁，母亲Jane de Timperley31岁。他和Johanna de Coghall至少有12个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1403年7月21日在英国英格兰史洛普爾史福斯波利享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Massey de Pontington was born about 1365, in Puddington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hamon de Massey, was 34 and his mother, Jane de Timperley, was 31. He had at least 12 sons and 1 daughter with Johanna de Coghall. He died on 21 July 1403, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Massey de Pontington est né environ 1365, à Puddington, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hamon de Massey, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Jane de Timperley, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 12 fils et 1 fille avec Johanna de Coghall. Il est décédé le 21 juillet 1403, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Massey de Pontington um 1365 in Puddington, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hamon de Massey 34 und seine Mutter Jane de Timperley 31. Er hatte mit Johanna de Coghall mindestens 12 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 21. Juli 1403 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Massey de Pontington è nato il circa 1365 nel Puddington, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hamon de Massey, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Jane de Timperley, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 12 figli e 1 figlia con Johanna de Coghall. Morì il 21 luglio 1403, nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Massey de Pontingtonが約1365年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアプウッドイントンで生まれた時，父Hamon de Masseyは34歳，母Jane de Timperleyは31歳であった。彼はJohanna de Coghallとの間に少なくとも12人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1403年7月21日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーで39歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Massey de Pontington(이)가 대략 1365년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 푸딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hamon de Massey(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane de Timperley(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Johanna de Coghall(와)과 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1403년 7월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Massey de Pontington родился около 1365 г. в местности пуддингтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hamon de Massey было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Jane de Timperley, 31.
